steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The main clause already has its verb: "a team of researchers ... analyzed geotagged social media posts." The blank introduces a modifier describing the researchers' purpose, not a second main verb.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
A modifier needs the participle hoping: "a team of researchers, hoping to find connections..., analyzed posts."

A, B, C all create a second finite verb, leaving the sentence with two main verbs and no modifier — ungrammatical.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
